campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council heard a financial report from the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority covering March 2026 investments, and adopted a new policy restricting the use of city property for federal civil enforcement activities. The council also discussed efforts to fill city job openings and improve employee retention.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New city policy prohibiting federal civil enforcement use of city property
City job vacancies and recruitment strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on April 15, 2026 to address objections to cleaning up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the County, and voted to move forward with the abatement work. The council also adopted a new technology policy and established a process for residents to request accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property abatement across the city
City Council approval to proceed with County-identified property cleanups
New Technology Disruption Policy adopted to replace existing policy
New process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes, review March financial reports, and authorize several projects and agreements. The council approved donations, service contracts, a road paving project with O'Grady Paving, and grant applications for statewide funding.
keyTopics: Approved road construction contract with O'Grady Paving
Authorized five-year service agreement
Accepted donation of automated external defibrillator
Submitted grant applications for statewide funding
Reviewed March 2026 financial and investment reports

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize upcoming agendas and work plans. The May 26 meeting was cancelled, so the council reviewed draft items for their June 2 meeting and updated their work schedules for neighborhood services, education, and government operations.
keyTopics: June 2, 2026 City Council meeting agenda being prepared
Updates to Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan review
Council-sponsored events requiring retroactive approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear public comments, and select members for the Youth Advisory Committee for the 2026-27 fiscal year. The council also reviewed upcoming agendas and reports from various boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee applicant selection for 2026-27
Public comment period
Board and commission agenda planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held its May 19, 2026 meeting to recognize Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month. The council reviewed April meeting minutes and conducted standard business at the 7 P.M. session.
keyTopics: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month recognition
Affordable Housing Month observance
April meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to update the city's Health and Safety plan that guides development through 2040, recognized the Public Works Department for their work on streets, parks, and infrastructure during Public Works Week, and celebrated Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Public Works Department recognition and services (streets, sidewalks, trees, facilities)
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month celebration
Some agenda items were postponed

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a special meeting of the Station Area Task Force on May 21, 2026, at City Hall to discuss planned development and improvements in the station area. The meeting was open to the public both in person and online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Station Area Task Force special meeting convened
Meeting held at City Hall, 1500 Warburton Avenue
Public participation available in-person and via Zoom

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they voted on several land development projects, passed new city rules, and appointed a permanent City Manager. The council conducted public hearings on zoning changes and a property assessment before making their final decisions.
keyTopics: Appointment of a new permanent City Manager
New zoning ordinance rules (urgency and standard ordinances)
Site development permit and tentative map approval for a development project
Property assessment confirmation hearing

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council received an update on Palo Alto's residential electrification efforts and discussed new principles to guide programs that help the city meet air quality regulations. Staff presented information on the status of these initiatives and how they'll work to reduce pollution from homes.
keyTopics: Residential electrification program status and progress
New proposed principles for electrification assistance programs
Air District regulation compliance requirements
Staff recommendations and next steps
